source: PDF shows [¶9] as separate paragraph starting after [¶8]; all other paragraph transitions in DB have proper spacing between period and next paragraph marker
evidence: Comparison of all [¶N] transitions in DB shows consistent pattern: '.<newline(s)>[¶' or (when merged) '.<space>[¶'. The [¶8]-[¶9] transition is the only one with '.[¶' (no space). Visual inspection of PDF page 4 confirms [¶9] begins new paragraph.
note: All other character differences between DB and PDF are intentional quote style differences (straight ASCII in DB vs. curly/typographic in PDF). This whitespace defect is the only substantive ingestion/OCR error.

source: PDF /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2022/2022ND130.pdf shows proper right-aligned indentation with 5 spaces before each justice's name on the signature page
note: The justice names in the signature block are indented to align vertically. The DB contains only 1 space before each name, while the PDF source shows 5 spaces for proper alignment. This is a presentation/mechanical whitespace error, not a content error.
